seed: control vs surrender; overflow_vs_containment; overfilled_center; stutter_step; hinge against negative_cross; thick_outline; pressure_vs_release; compass; lonely_signal | abstract variation request 250; target batch count 500
title: Tension Between Control and Surrender
reasoning: The emotional tone reflects the struggle between the rigid bounds of control and the flowing nature of surrender. The palette incorporates soft pastels of powder pink and warm white to evoke gentle yielding, contrasted with deeper hues like carbon black and sharp crimson to represent the intensity of control. The primary collision occurs in the center, marked by jagged edges that interrupt the rhythmic pattern, symbolizing the tensions in navigating emotion. The barcode pattern, representing control, and the ripple pattern of surrender ripple outward against a backdrop of quiet black and gray tones, creating a visual tension that reflects the core struggle in the seed. Broken pixels are scattered to symbolize imperfections, introducing a sense of uncertainty and disruption throughout the composition.

layout.name: scattered_repair
layout.description: Broken marks are patched by brighter pixels that form an incomplete repair path.

composition.name: order_vs_chaos
composition.idea: One side follows a clean pattern while the other breaks apart.

referenceExample.title: Static Around a Bridge
referenceExample.pattern: cold broken islands interrupted by a warm connecting channel

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
